## Idea
# Vancouver Dinner

I want to have dinner on Friday, March 27, 2026 in Vancouver with 5-10 people. They all live in random places around the Greater Vancouver Area.

I need different options for places to go, how to reserve, what to say, who to call, when to call, etc. The dinner must cater to dietary restrictions and be centrally located with easy transit options.



## Prior Work on the Blackboard
The following files already exist for this idea. Read the ones relevant to your role before planning your work.

- `artifact-manifest.json` (json, 0.1 KB)
- `dinner-brief.html` (html, 13.2 KB)
- `idea.md` (md, 0.4 KB)
- `organizer-dashboard.html` (html, 50.3 KB)
- `restaurant-options.html` (html, 33.3 KB)

Use `read_blackboard` to read any file above. Build on prior work — don't duplicate it.

## Global Agent Learnings

## event-fundraiser-validation
## Get Baked & Watch The Matrix — 4-Cycle Validation Learnings

### Idea Summary
Cannabis-friendly trans rights fundraiser film screening of The Matrix (confirmed trans allegory) in Nelson BC, Canada. Raises money via door donations, raffle (dispensary-donated prizes), cash sponsorships, merch, and pre-event online donations.

### Verified Facts (4 cycles of research)
1. **The Matrix IS a confirmed trans allegory** — Lilly Wachowski confirmed in 2020 interview
2. **Criterion Pictures Canada** = correct vendor for Warner Bros. non-theatrical Canadian film licenses (NOT Swank, which is US). Call 1-800-565-1996. Cost $150-450 CAD for <200 attendees.
3. **BC Cannabis law**: No indoor smoking/vaping at events with staff. "Arrive elevated" framing is legally correct and culturally authentic. Outdoor venues (Gyro Park) allow on-site consumption.
4. **Nelson Pride founded 1996** — first Pride outside a major city in North America. 2026 = 30th anniversary. Pride Week typically late August.
5. **Nelson dispensaries**: Woody Nelson, The Potorium, Buddy's Place — all downtown, community-minded.
6. **BC Gaming raffle license**: Partner with established nonprofit (Nelson Pride Society) — organizers cannot apply directly without 12+ months operating history. Class B license = $25 fee, 10 business days.
7. **Nano Banana**: REAL tool — Google Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model. Available at nanobanana.art, nano-banana.ai, Pixlr, OpenArt, ImagineArt. Free credits available.

### Revenue Model (Validated)
- Conservative net: $1,195-1,395 (80 attendees, $10 avg donation)
- Realistic net: $2,775-2,900 (100 attendees, $15 avg donation)
- Optimistic net: $5,600-6,100 (150 attendees, $20 avg donation)
- **Iowa film fundraiser precedent**: $10,000+ raised in single trans rights screening in politically hostile environment → Nelson BC (progressive, 30-year Pride history) could exceed this

### Critical Human Decisions Still Required
1. Event date: April 20, 2026 (40-day lead time, tight) vs August 24, 2026 (Nelson Pride Week, 30th anniversary, recommended)
2. Beneficiary organization: Recommend 70% Egale Canada + 30% Nelson Pride Society trans programming fund
3. Contact Nelson Pride Society for raffle license partnership

### Deliverables Created (All HTML)
- research.html — market analysis
- competitive-analysis.html — event landscape
- feasibility.html — risk/budget dashboard with 3-scenario model
- marketing-website.html — production-ready event/host-your-own website with Matrix rain animation
- marketing-prompts.html — 21 AI art prompts for all marketing collateral
- implementation-showcase.html — master document with speech drafts
- implementation-log.html — execution timeline
- release-plan.html — timing decision framework + deployment guide
- social-media-calendar.html — NEW in Cycle 4: 8-week content calendar, 20 pre-written captions, hashtag library, platform specs
- refinement-notes.html — all corrections and validations across 4 cycles
- validation-report.html — formal validation document

### Lessons for Future Similar Ideas
- Cannabis event regulations vary widely by jurisdiction — always verify indoor consumption rules first
- Film licensing in Canada goes through different vendors than US (Criterion vs Swank)
- Community fundraiser raffle licenses typically require an established nonprofit partner
- "Arrive elevated" is legally correct framing for indoor cannabis events in BC
- Split beneficiary (national org + local org) tells a better story than either alone
- Social media content calendar is always a missing deliverable — create it proactively
- Comparable events validation is critical: always search for precedents to calibrate revenue projections
- Nelson BC is unusually progressive for its size — community engagement will be strong


## REFINEMENT MODE (cycle #1)
This idea has been through 4 iterations and released 1 time(s). You are now in REFINEMENT mode.

**Your job is NOT to redo your work from scratch.** Instead:
1. Read ALL existing artifacts on the blackboard using `list_files` and `read_blackboard`
2. Critically analyze your previous outputs — find weaknesses, blind spots, unstated assumptions
3. Do NEW research to validate or disprove claims in existing artifacts
4. Scrutinize numbers, projections, and estimates against real-world data
5. IMPROVE existing artifacts by overwriting them with better versions
6. Write a brief refinement note to your knowledge file using `write_knowledge`

Think like a devil's advocate. Challenge everything. Make it bulletproof.


Now execute your role for idea 'vancouver-dinner'. Read any prior artifacts listed above before planning your work. Use the blackboard tools to write your outputs. When done, use `declare_artifacts` to register what you created, then `set_phase_recommendation` to indicate what should happen next.

content.text:
Vancouver Group Dinner

Friday, March 27, 2026

5–10 people · Dietary-inclusive · Central transit access · Greater Vancouver
TODAY IS MARCH 13 — YOU HAVE 14 DAYS. RESERVE NOW. MOST DOWNTOWN RESTAURANTS FILL FRIDAYS BY TUESDAY OF THAT WEEK.
Action Timeline
TODAY — Mar 13
Call your top pick
Phone reservations for groups are always better than online. Mention party size, date, time, and dietary needs.
Mar 14 — Sat
Confirm headcount
Text guests, get a firm yes/no. Most restaurants need final numbers 48–72 hrs ahead.
Mar 20 — Fri
Collect dietary info
Ask guests for allergies and restrictions. Share with the restaurant when you call to confirm.
Mar 25 — Wed
Call to confirm
Re-confirm final headcount and any dietary requirements. Ask about parking or late arrivals.
Mar 27 — Fri
Dinner night
Arrive together or stagger — confirm a meeting point if guests are coming by different transit routes.
Transit Overview — Getting Everyone There

All four restaurants are within 5–10 minutes walk of a major SkyTrain station. Downtown Vancouver stations serve guests coming from North Van (SeaBus), Burnaby, Coquitlam, Surrey (Expo/Millennium), and Richmond/Airport (Canada Line).

Restaurant Options
Chambar Top Pick
Belgian-inspired, globally-influenced — Downtown Vancouver
$$$ — ~$60–85/person
Address
568 Beatty St, Vancouver
Near BC Place & Rogers Arena
Phone
604-879-7119
Booking Method
Phone or OpenTable for under 12 guests. Large party form for 12+.
Hours (Fri)
Dinner from 5:30 PM. Reserve 6:30 or 7:00 PM for group.
Dietary
Vegetarian Gluten-Free Vegan options
Kitchen accommodates allergies. Notify when booking.
Private Space
Small private room for up to 20 seated. Ideal for a group of 8–10.
Stadium–Chinatown 4 min walk via Beatty St — Expo Line, Millennium Line
North Shore guests: SeaBus to Waterfront, then Expo Line 1 stop to Stadium. Richmond guests: Canada Line to Waterfront or transfer. Burnaby/Surrey/Coquitlam: Expo/Millennium direct.
Nuba Gastown Alt Pick
Lebanese, plant-forward — Gastown
$$ — ~$30–45/person
Address
207 W Hastings St, Vancouver
Heart of Gastown
Phone
604-688-1655
Booking Method
Phone preferred for groups. Also on OpenTable.
Hours (Fri)
Dinner from 5:00 PM. Book 6:30 PM or later.
Dietary
Vegetarian Gluten-Free Mostly Vegan Halal-friendly
Large portion of menu is vegan and GF. Kitchen is not dedicated GF — note for severe celiac.
Private Space
No private room, but can arrange reserved section for groups.
Waterfront Station 7 min walk — Expo, Canada, Millennium Lines + SeaBus terminus
Best single station for the whole GVA. SeaBus from North Shore lands directly here. All SkyTrain lines converge at Waterfront.
Earls Kitchen + Bar — Yaletown Casual Pick
American / Canadian, polished casual — Yaletown
$$ — ~$35–55/person
Address
1095 Mainland St, Vancouver
Yaletown, near seawall
Phone
604-684-4990
Booking Method
Phone or OpenTable. Groups of 7+ call directly.
Hours (Fri)
Open from 11:30 AM. Dinner any time after 5:30 PM.
Dietary
Vegetarian Gluten-Free Vegan options
Menu labels GF and vegetarian items clearly. Allergy-aware kitchen.
Private Space
No private room. Booth seating accommodates groups well. All-season patio.
Yaletown–Roundhouse 5 min walk — Canada Line (direct from Richmond, Airport, South Van)
Best for guests coming from Richmond, YVR, or South Vancouver. Burnaby/Coquitlam guests transfer at Waterfront or use Expo+bus.
CRAFT Beer Market — Olympic Village Lively Pick
Canadian pub-style, large format — Olympic Village
$$ — ~$30–50/person
Address
85 W 1st Ave, Vancouver
Olympic Village, False Creek
Phone
604-709-2337
Booking Method
Phone or OpenTable. Large groups very welcome here.
Hours (Fri)
Open 11:30 AM until late. Great for a longer evening.
Dietary
Vegetarian Gluten-Free Some Vegan
Good variety but least diet-forward of the four. Confirm specific needs when booking.
Atmosphere
Loud, lively, great for a social group. 100+ beers on tap. Not for quiet conversation.
Olympic Village 8 min walk — Canada Line (2 stops from Waterfront)
Good for Canada Line riders. Expo Line guests: Waterfront then 2 stops south. North Shore: SeaBus + Canada Line. Slightly less convenient for east side guests.
Quick Comparison
Restaurant Price Vegan-Friendly GF-Friendly Private Room Noise Level Nearest Station OpenTable?
Chambar $$$ Yes Yes Yes (20 pax) Moderate Stadium–Chinatown (4 min) Yes
Nuba Gastown $$ Largely vegan Mostly GF* No Moderate Waterfront (7 min) Yes
Earls Yaletown $$ Some options Yes No Moderate-High Yaletown–Roundhouse (5 min) Yes
CRAFT Olympic Village $$ Some options Some options No Loud Olympic Village (8 min) Yes

* Nuba GF note: dishes are made without gluten but kitchen is not certified GF. Not safe for severe celiac.

Reservation Scripts

Calling for a group of 8–10

Phone Script — Standard Group Booking
"Hi, I'd like to make a reservation for a group of [8 or 10] people for dinner on Friday, March 27th. We're flexible on time between 6:30 and 7:30 PM — what works best for you?

We do have a few dietary restrictions in the group. We have [e.g., one vegetarian, one gluten-free, one dairy-free]. Can your kitchen accommodate that?

Great. And is there any chance of a semi-private section or a table together? We'd love to be able to hear each other.

Could I give you a name and a phone number to hold that? My name is [Your Name], and my number is [Your Number].

Perfect. And do you have a cancellation policy for large groups? We'll confirm final numbers by Wednesday the 25th."
If they ask for a credit card to hold a large-group reservation, that is normal. Ask about the cancellation window (usually 24–48 hours).

If they are fully booked

Phone Script — Waitlist / Fallback
"Oh, that's too bad. Is there any chance of a cancellation? Could you put us on a waitlist?

And do you know if any of your other locations might have availability that evening?"
If Chambar is full, call Nuba next (604-688-1655), then Earls (604-684-4990), then CRAFT (604-709-2337). You can also book via OpenTable to check availability in real time.

Confirming dietary restrictions when you call back (March 25)

Confirmation Call Script
"Hi, I have a reservation for [Your Name], a group of [final number] on Friday, March 27th. I'm calling to confirm and to let you know about our dietary needs.

We have: [list specific restrictions: e.g., one person with a severe tree nut allergy, two vegetarians, one person who is gluten-sensitive but not celiac].

Is that something the kitchen can manage? Great. We'll see you Friday."
Practical Tips

Headcount discipline

Most restaurants charge a no-show fee per head for large groups. Get a firm commitment from each guest before you give the restaurant a number. 8 confirmed is better than 10 with 2 maybes.

Time of arrival

Friday nights in downtown Vancouver are busy. Book 6:30 or 7:00 PM to avoid the post-work rush at 6:00 PM. Tell guests to arrive by 6:20 so you're seated together at the reserved time.

Transit tip for guests

Share the station and walk directions with each guest when you confirm. A simple "take Expo Line to Stadium-Chinatown, then walk south on Beatty" is enough. Compass Card accepted on all TransLink services.

Dietary communication

Ask guests to share their restrictions now, not the day before. If anyone has a severe allergy (nut, shellfish), notify the restaurant at booking and again on the confirmation call, not just when you arrive.

Shared bill vs. split

Call ahead and ask if they can do multiple splits or if they prefer one bill. Most downtown Vancouver restaurants can split up to 6 ways easily; more requires advance notice.

Backup plan

Book your first choice today. If that falls through close to the date, CRAFT Beer Market is your best walk-in fallback — they handle large groups without reservations better than the others.

Reservation Scripts

Calling for a group of 8–10 (Chambar or Earls)

Phone Script — Standard Group Booking
"Hi, I'd like to make a reservation for a group of [8 or 10] people for dinner on Friday, March 27th. We're flexible on time between 6:30 and 7:30 PM — what works best for you?

We have a few dietary restrictions in the group: [e.g., one vegetarian, one gluten-free, one dairy-free]. Can your kitchen accommodate that?

Is there any chance of a semi-private section or a table together? We'd love to be able to hear each other.

Could I give you a name and phone number to hold that? My name is [Your Name], my number is [Your Number].

And just to confirm — do you have a cancellation policy for large groups? We'll confirm final numbers by Wednesday the 25th."
If they ask for a credit card to hold a large-group reservation, that is standard. Ask about the cancellation window (usually 24–48 hours). At Chambar, for 12 or more people, you need to fill out the Large Party Request Form at chambar.com first.

Emailing for Nuba Gastown (groups of 8 or more)

Email to nubagastown@gmail.com
Subject: Group reservation request — March 27, 2026

"Hi,

I'm hoping to book a dinner for [8 or 10] people at Nuba Gastown on Friday, March 27th. We'd like to dine around 6:30 or 7:00 PM.

We have the following dietary needs in the group: [e.g., three people who are vegan, one who is gluten-free, one with a nut allergy].

Could you let me know if you have availability, and whether you can accommodate a reserved section for the group?

Thank you,
[Your Name]
[Your phone number]"
Nuba does not use OpenTable. Use Resy for smaller bookings, or email for groups. Their reply time is typically within a few hours during business hours.

If your first choice is fully booked

Phone Script — Waitlist / Fallback
"Oh, that's too bad. Is there any chance of a cancellation by next Friday? Could you add us to a waitlist?

And if anything opens up by Tuesday the 24th, could you give me a call?"
Call order if your first choice is full: Chambar (604-879-7119) → Miku (email info@mikurestaurant.com) → Nuba (email nubagastown@gmail.com) → Earls (604-688-4990) → CRAFT (604-709-2337). CRAFT is the most reliable walk-in fallback for a group this size.

Confirmation call (Wednesday, March 25)

Confirmation Call Script
"Hi, I have a reservation for [Your Name], a group of [final number] on Friday, March 27th. I'm calling to confirm and to share our final dietary needs.

We have: [list specific restrictions, e.g., one person with a severe tree nut allergy, two vegans, one person who is gluten-sensitive but not celiac].

Is that something the kitchen can manage? Great. And can you split the bill multiple ways? We'll see you Friday."
Practical Tips

Headcount discipline

Most restaurants charge a per-head no-show fee for large groups. Get a firm commitment from each guest before giving the restaurant a number. Eight confirmed is better than ten with two maybes.

Arrival time

Friday nights in downtown Vancouver are busy. Book 6:30 or 7:00 PM to avoid the post-work rush at 6:00 PM. Tell guests to arrive by 6:20 so you are all seated together on time.

Transit tip for guests

Share station and walk directions with each guest when you confirm. "Take Expo Line to Stadium-Chinatown, then walk south on Beatty for 4 minutes" is all anyone needs. Compass Card works on all TransLink services.

Communicate dietary needs now

Ask guests for restrictions today, not the day before. If anyone has a severe allergy (nut, shellfish, dairy), tell the restaurant at booking and repeat it on the confirmation call. Saying "I'm allergic to tree nuts and it can be serious" is more actionable than a vague note.

Splitting the bill

Ask when you book whether they can do multiple bill splits. Most downtown Vancouver restaurants split up to 6 ways without trouble. More than that requires advance notice. Mention it when you call so there's no awkward conversation at the end of the night.

Backup plan

Book your first choice today. If that falls through close to the date, CRAFT Beer Market is the most forgiving walk-in option for a group this size — they handle large parties without reservations better than the rest.
